Per the settled model (procurement owns delivery, site owns р╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З), consolidated all delivery into ONE **р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З** block. **New** `PoDeliverySection` (server-safe): heading р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З тЖТ ETA/status line (р╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З <date> / р╕кр╣Ир╕Зр╕Др╕гр╕Ър╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з / р╕гр╕нр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З) тЖТ the р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З **branch breakdown** when the PO forked (р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Чр╕╡р╣И N ┬╖ count ┬╖ р╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з <date> тЬУ + р╕Др╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕кр╣Ир╕З ┬╖ count ┬╖ р╕Др╕▓р╕Ф <eta> тП│) тЖТ **р╕лр╕ер╕▒р╕Бр╕Рр╕▓р╕Щр╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З** (the proof_of_delivery gallery + uploader, procurement attaches). **Page:** removed the summary ETA line, the standalone breakdown render, and the inline р╕лр╕ер╕▒р╕Бр╕Рр╕▓р╕Щр╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З section; renders `<PoDeliverySection>` after the tracker, before the р╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З checklist; deleted the now-orphaned `po-delivery-breakdown.tsx` (inlined). Order: summary+tracker тЖТ **р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З** (delivery home: ETA ┬╖ branches ┬╖ proof) тЖТ **р╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З** (site receive action, U8-gated) тЖТ line list. `р╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З` stays the site's action; р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З is procurement's delivery view. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; groupDeliveryBatches carries the tests). **Spec 134 = U1тАУU9 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove) parked on creds.

## Spec 135 U1 - First-class deliveries: data layer + auto-create + backfill (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migrations `20260718000000_purchase_order_deliveries` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **62 files / 1309 / 0** (new file 62 = 14, incl the backfill invariant on real data); db:types reconciled (61eb1cf); app typecheck clean (additive тАФ no app code touches deliveries yet). **Additive by design** тАФ leaves U7 `delivery_batch_id` + the receive/split RPCs untouched so the U9 reads keep working until U2. **Table** `purchase_order_deliveries` (po_id, eta, note, cost [money/back-office], carrier [future Lalamove], created_by) + RLS (back-office SELECT mirroring purchase_orders; NO direct write тАФ RPC-only, ADR 0038) + `purchase_requests.delivery_id` FK (column-scope grant excludes it тЖТ RPC-only). **Auto-create:** `create_purchase_order` (CREATE OR REPLACE of the 20260701000300 body + the delivery block; same 5-arg signature) now inserts the default delivery (= whole PO) and stamps every member line's `delivery_id` тЖТ every PO has тЙе1 delivery, the 85% never touch it. **Backfill** (DO block): one delivery per (PO, delivery_batch_id) group + the default/remainder; pgTAP proves no PO-member line is left without a delivery. **Verified-by-pgTAP** (no app/UI change in U1). **Next:** U2 = р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З section renders the deliveries list (switch the app off delivery_batch_id тЖТ delivery_id); U3 procurement split-delivery UI; U4 per-delivery proof. Spec 134 U5/U7/U9 will re-point at deliveries; delivery_batch_id retired in a later migration once the app is off it.

## Spec 135 U2 - р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З renders the deliveries list; split inherits its delivery (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migration `20260719000000_split_inherits_delivery` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **62 files / 1310 / 0** (file 60 now 27 тАФ the inherit assertion; backfill invariant still 0). The PO detail now reads the real `purchase_order_deliveries` (U1) instead of the U7 receipt-batch grouping. **App:** pure `buildDeliveriesView(deliveries, lines)` тЖТ per-delivery {ordinal р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Чр╕╡р╣И N (by created_at), eta, derived status (reuse derivePurchaseOrderStatus over the delivery's lines), active lineCount, latest receivedAt} (5 unit tests, TDD); `PoDeliverySection` renders ONE delivery (the 85% default) as a calm status line and MULTIPLE as the р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З list (р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Чр╕╡р╣И N ┬╖ status pill ┬╖ eta ┬╖ count ┬╖ receipt тЬУ). Page reads `purchase_order_deliveries` + groups members by `delivery_id` (was delivery_batch_id); **deleted** `groupDeliveryBatches` + its test (replaced). **Correctness migration:** the within-ticket split (U3) left its remainder child with `delivery_id` NULL after U1 (orphan); now the child INHERITS `v_orig.delivery_id` (ADR 0054 ┬з7) тАФ file 60 proves it. cost deferred to U3 (money). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page; helper + RPC carry tests). delivery_batch_id is now app-dead (still written vestigially by receive/split; retired in a later migration). **Next:** U3 procurement split-delivery UI (sheet: select lines + eta + cost) + RPC; U4 per-delivery proof.

## Spec 135 U3 - Procurement split-delivery UI + guarded RPC (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migrations `20260720000000_split_po_delivery` + fix-forward `20260720000100_split_po_delivery_fix_lock` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **63 files / 1326 / 0** (new file 63 = 16); db:types zero drift (CREATE OR REPLACE, same signature). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **979 unit** (incl. 5 new TDD) ┬╖ green. **Caught by pgTAP:** the first RPC used `count(*) тАж FOR UPDATE`, which Postgres rejects (0A000) тАФ every real split errored; fix-forward locks the selected rows in a separate `PERFORM тАж FOR UPDATE` then counts.

## Spec 135 U4 - Per-delivery proof of delivery (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migration `20260721000000_po_attachment_delivery_id` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **63 files / 1331 / 0** (file 50 now 34, +5: delivery_id col + view col + FK violation + back-office insert + view surfaces); db:types reconciled (the two delivery_id FK Relationships entries, app+worker). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **984 unit** (+5 new TDD) ┬╖ green. **Closes the spec 135 deliveries arc.** A proof-of-delivery (signed note / received-goods photo) now belongs to the **delivery (р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф)** it documents, not the whole PO. **Migration** (mirrors the 20260713000000 purpose migration): `purchase_order_attachments.delivery_id` nullable FK тЖТ `purchase_order_deliveries(id)` + index + `grant insert(delivery_id)` (ADR 0038 column-scope) + recreate `purchase_order_attachments_current` to carry it. **NO backfill** тАФ the table is append-only (the block-write trigger rejects UPDATE), so legacy proof (`delivery_id` NULL) surfaces under the PO's **default** (earliest, р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Чр╕╡р╣И 1) delivery via the app helper, not a data migration. **App:** `addProofOfDeliveryAttachment` now takes a `deliveryId`, **validated to belong to the PO** (server-side read under RLS) before insert, and stamps it; `ProofOfDeliveryUploader` carries `deliveryId`; pure `groupProofByDelivery(docs, defaultDeliveryId)` (5 unit tests, TDD) maps each doc to its delivery (NULL тЖТ default); `PoDeliverySection` renders proof **per delivery** тАФ one block for the 85% single-delivery default (unchanged feel), a per-р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф block (heading "р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Чр╕╡р╣И N ┬╖ р╕лр╕ер╕▒р╕Бр╕Рр╕▓р╕Щр╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З" + its own uploader) when the PO is split. Page query adds `delivery_id`, groups, passes `proofByDelivery`. The delivery-belongs-to-PO RLS with-check left as a possible follow-up (action enforces it; the INSERT policy is unchanged, mirroring the purpose migration's minimalism). **Verified-by-checklist** for the UI (auth-gated; helper + pgTAP carry tests). **Spec 135 = U1┬╖U2┬╖U3┬╖U4 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove auto-POD + per-delivery cost) parked on sandbox creds тАФ the delivery entity + per-delivery proof/cost are its join point.

## Spec 135 U5 - Delivery detail page; р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З = one action (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). **App/nav only тАФ no migration** (reuses U4's `delivery_id`); no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **988 unit** (+3 new TDD) ┬╖ green. **Operator UX:** the р╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З section had **two** buttons (р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З + р╣Бр╕Щр╕Ър╕лр╕ер╕▒р╕Бр╕Рр╕▓р╕Щ per delivery, multiplied per р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф after U4). Consolidated to one. **New route** `src/app/requests/orders/[poId]/deliveries/[deliveryId]/page.tsx` тАФ a delivery detail page (PURCHASING_ROLES gate, uuid-guard + notFound, delivery must belong to the PO): р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф summary (derived status pill via buildDeliveriesView ┬╖ eta ┬╖ cost [back-office display gate, money] ┬╖ note), its member lines (link to the PR), and **р╕лр╕ер╕▒р╕Бр╕Рр╕▓р╕Щр╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З** (the U4 gallery + uploader, scoped to this delivery; legacy NULL proof under the default р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф via groupProofByDelivery). **`PoDeliverySection` redesign:** every delivery row is now a **Link** to its detail page (chevron affordance; single + multi alike), the inline proof block is **removed**, only the р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З button stays тЖТ one action. **PO detail page:** dropped the proof query + mintSignedUrls + groupProofByDelivery + the proof props (proof now lives on the delivery page). **New nav SSOT** `src/lib/nav/order-paths.ts` тАФ `poDetailHref` / `deliveryDetailHref` (3 unit tests, TDD; mirrors project-paths). **Extracted** `DeliveryProofBlock` to its own component (off po-delivery-section, used by the detail page). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pages; nav helper carries the test). **Spec 135 = U1┬╖U2┬╖U3┬╖U4┬╖U5 on prod;** only U4b parked on creds.

## Spec 135 U6 - Delivery dispatch + branching tracker + date fix (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17 (ADR 0054). Migration `20260722000000_dispatch_po_delivery` merged + db:push'd (dry-run clean); db:test **64 files / 1341 / 0** (new file 64 = 10); db:types zero drift. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **988 unit** ┬╖ green. **From the operator's device test (3 findings):** (a) **date overflow** тАФ the split-sheet eta `<input type="date">` overflowed on iOS; added `min-w-0 appearance-none` (the purchase-record-form pattern). (b) **stuck at р╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕нр╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з** тАФ `on_route` (тЖТ in_transit / р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З) was only reachable via the per-ticket `record_shipment` (buried on the PR detail); the operator at the delivery level had no path. **New RPC `dispatch_purchase_order_delivery(p_delivery_id)`** (back-office gate, mirrors record_shipment: sets the р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф's `purchased` lines' `shipped_at` тЖТ the existing on_route derive + audit trigger chain flips them, **no new trigger**; returns the count, re-dispatch = 0-row no-op) + `dispatchPurchaseOrderDelivery` action + `DeliveryDispatchControl` "р╕Ър╕▒р╕Щр╕Чр╕╢р╕Бр╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З" button on the delivery detail page (back-office, shown when the р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф has purchased lines). (c) **tracker didn't branch** тАФ `PoDeliveriesTracker` renders a per-р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф `PurchaseOrderTracker` (each its own р╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕нтЖТр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕ЗтЖТр╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З from its derived status, left-ruled) on the PO detail when `deliveries.length > 1`; single-delivery keeps the one linear tracker. **Verified-by-checklist** for button/tracker/date (auth-gated; RPC carries the pgTAP). **Spec 135 = U1┬╖U2┬╖U3┬╖U4┬╖U5┬╖U6 on prod;** only U4b (Lalamove auto-dispatch тАФ fills the same on_route transition automatically) parked on creds. Procurement plans a PO's deliveries (р╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З): the explicit split for the multi-delivery 15% тАФ the U1 default delivery keeps the 85% one-tap. **Migration `20260720000000_split_po_delivery`:** new `split_purchase_order_delivery(p_purchase_order_id, p_request_ids uuid[], p_eta?, p_note?, p_cost?)` SECURITY DEFINER RPC (the only writer of `purchase_order_deliveries` + `delivery_id` besides create_purchase_order, ADR 0038). Creates a NEW delivery carrying eta/note/cost and repoints the selected lines' `delivery_id`. **Gate:** back office (project_manager/procurement/super_admin) тАФ **NOT site_admin** (a delivery is procurement's PLAN, not a р╕гр╕▒р╕Ър╕Вр╕нр╕З receive action, U8). **Guards:** every selected id is a distinct in-transit (`purchased`/`on_route`) member of the PO (one count check catches non-member, delivered/rejected/cancelled, and dup ids); no **source delivery emptied** тАФ each touched delivery keeps тЙе1 active line (a delivered line counts, so it keeps a source alive after all its in-transit lines move); cost тЙе 0. One split audit row (action `update`, target `purchase_order_deliveries`, payload `transition:['delivery_split']` тАФ ADR 0027/0031 no-new-enum precedent). **App:** `splitPurchaseOrderDelivery` action (relays the RPC); pure `deliverySplitWouldEmptySource(selected, activeCountByDelivery)` (5 unit tests, TDD) mirrors the non-empty guard for a clear inline message; `SplitDeliveryControl` client component тАФ a "р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Зр╕зр╕Фр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З" trigger тЖТ BottomSheet (spec 78): line checkboxes + р╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З (date) + р╕Др╣Ир╕▓р╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З (money) + р╕лр╕бр╕▓р╕вр╣Ар╕лр╕Хр╕╕ тЖТ submit. `PoDeliverySection` renders the trigger only when back office AND some delivery still holds тЙе2 active lines (a split is possible); page passes `splittableLines` (in-transit members) + `activeCountByDelivery`. **Verified-by-checklist** for the UI (auth-gated; RPC carries the pgTAP). cost now lands (the U2 deferral). **UX refinement (2026-06-17, operator):** the split sheet opens with **all lines pre-ticked** (most deliveries carry the whole order тАФ untick only the items going to another р╕Зр╕зр╕Ф), re-seeded on every open; a proactive "р╕Хр╣Йр╕нр╕Зр╣Ар╕лр╕ер╕╖р╕нр╕нр╕вр╣Ир╕▓р╕Зр╕Щр╣Йр╕нр╕в 1 р╕гр╕▓р╕вр╕Бр╕▓р╕гр╣Др╕зр╣Йр╕нр╕╡р╕Бр╕Зр╕зр╕Ф" hint replaces the silently-disabled submit when all are still ticked (can't move the whole source out). **Next:** U4 per-delivery proof (`purchase_order_attachments.delivery_id`; scope the POD uploader/gallery to a delivery).

## Spec 136 - Create a purchase request on the WP page only (+ PM self-approve) (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17. **App-only, no migration** (self-approval already works at the data layer); no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **988 unit** ┬╖ green. **Operator:** "the р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Др╕│р╕Вр╕нр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н section on the р╕Др╕│р╕Вр╕нр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н (/requests) tab should be removed тАФ we don't allow users to make a request there anyway." **Removed** the р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Др╕│р╕Вр╕нр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н section from `/requests` + the now-dead `?wp=` machinery (pinned-WP resolution, the spec-12 contextual back-bar, the `?wp=`-preserving filter-chip hrefs, the unused imports). `/requests` is purely the worklist now. **Catch found via grep:** the PM **review** WP page (`/review/work-packages/[id]`) created a request by a cross-tab "р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Др╕│р╕Вр╕нр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н тЖТ" link INTO that section (the only live entry); the SA project WP page already creates inline (spec 29). So **moved PM creation inline** тАФ replaced the link with the shared `PurchaseRequestForm` in a `<details>` (mirrors the project WP page). `/requests?wp=` is fully dead now. **PM self-approve (operator ask):** `PurchaseRequestForm` gains `canSelfApprove` (PM/super) тЖТ the submit becomes **"р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╣Бр╕ер╕░р╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤"**, which after create (+ attachment flush) chains `decidePurchaseRequest(approved)` so a PM-raised request is approved in one tap (a PM is the approver). SA keeps the plain "р╕кр╣Ир╕Зр╕Др╕│р╕Вр╕нр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н". **No migration:** `decidePurchaseRequest` is a user-session UPDATE gated only by `status='requested'` тАФ no requesterтЙаapprover guard, so self-approval already works (approved_by + the audit trigger record the actor). Confirmation reads "р╕Ър╕▒р╕Щр╕Чр╕╢р╕Бр╣Бр╕ер╕░р╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤р╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з"; if the approve leg fails the request stays pending (approvable from the worklist) and an inline note says so. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pages; the form-render unit test still passes). Acceptance owed: raise a request from a WP as SA тЖТ pending; as PM тЖТ "р╕кр╕гр╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╣Бр╕ер╕░р╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤" тЖТ approved; /requests shows no create section.

## Spec 137 - Action-state bands + view filter on the site /requests worklist (2026-06-17)

Status: COMPLETE on prod тАФ 2026-06-17. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **995 unit** (+7 new TDD) ┬╖ green. **Operator:** site people need more than р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Ф/р╕Вр╕нр╕Зр╕Йр╕▒р╕Щ, the flat sort "is not intuitive", and "at the very least be able to filter out items received." (I recommended + built; aligns with the locked SA action-state lens, [[worklist-priority-alignment]].) The non-procurement `/requests` view (SA/PM/super) was a flat list (pending by priority, then everything newest-first) + a р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Ф/р╕Вр╕нр╕Зр╕Йр╕▒р╕Щ toggle. **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/request-bands.ts` (7 unit tests, TDD): `requestBand(status)` тЖТ action-state band; `groupRequestsByBand(rows, view)` тЖТ ordered non-empty bands **р╕гр╕нр╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤** (requested) тЖТ **р╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤р╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н** (approved) тЖТ **р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З** (purchased/on_route) тЖТ **р╣Ар╕кр╕гр╣Зр╕Ир╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з** (delivered/site_purchased) тЖТ **р╣Др╕бр╣Ир╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤/р╕вр╕Бр╣Ар╕ер╕┤р╕Б** (rejected/cancelled), active bands sorted priority-then-oldest (`comparePendingRequests`), done/closed newest-first; `RequestView` active(default)/done/all with `VIEW_BANDS` тАФ **active HIDES received+closed** (the operator's minimum). **Page:** `?view=` (default active); the non-procurement filter row is now a **view segmented control** (р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Фр╕│р╣Ар╕Щр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕▓р╕г/р╣Ар╕кр╕гр╣Зр╕Ир╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з/р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Ф) + a **р╣Ар╕Йр╕Юр╕▓р╕░р╕Вр╕нр╕Зр╕Йр╕▒р╕Щ** toggle (replaces the old р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Ф/р╕Вр╕нр╕Зр╕Йр╕▒р╕Щ 2-chip; each preserves the other axis via `reqHref`, defaults omitted from the URL); the list renders as **band sections** (heading + count + cards), 3-way empty state (no requests / none in this view / bands). No new query (bands computed over the already-fetched pending+decided rows). **Procurement view unchanged** (keeps its spec-104 pipeline + spec-110 filters). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated; the band helper carries the tests). Spec doc `137-site-worklist-bands.md`. Acceptance owed: site sees grouped bands, default hides received, view chips + р╣Ар╕Йр╕Юр╕▓р╕░р╕Вр╕нр╕Зр╕Йр╕▒р╕Щ work. **Consistency refinement (2026-06-17, operator: "check UI, not the same as the other side; think hard what users want to know"):** the site bands now match the procurement pipeline's section styling тАФ `hot` band (amber heading + amber count chip) where the site's attention band = **р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З** (incoming = what site receives, the analogue of procurement's buy-now band). And the thing site most wants to know тАФ **is anything late** тАФ surfaces as a `р╣Ар╕ер╕вр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф N` danger chip on a band with overdue arrivals (`groupRequestsByBand(rows, view, today)` counts items whose `eta` < today; +3 unit tests, 998 total). The shared `PurchaseRequestCard` already carries ETA/tracker/status per row.

## Spec 95 (hardening) - iOS keyboard-freeze: scroll-independent repaint for short forms (2026-06-17)

Status: code COMPLETE, **NOT device-verified** (desktop preview cannot reproduce the WebKit/iOS-PWA quirk тАФ operator must confirm on a real iOS PWA, especially a SHORT form). **App-only, no migration.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **999 unit** ┬╖ green. **Operator: "fix the frozen screen after closing the keyboard for good."** Recurrence #2 of the spec-95 blank-after-keyboard glitch (recurrence #1 = the still-focused-field case, fixed 436ee6f). **Root cause:** every `ViewportScrollGuard.repaintScroller` path forced the repaint via `scroller.scrollBy(0,1)` тАФ which only repaints when `<main>` can actually scroll. A form that fits the viewport (no overflow) can't scroll тЖТ scrollBy moves nothing тЖТ the locked scroller (spec 64: body overflow:hidden, `<main>` the only scroller) stays blank. The 85% of long forms recovered; short ones froze "for good". **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now ALSO does a scroll-INDEPENDENT repaint тАФ a 1px `transform: translateY(1px)` nudge on `<main>`, restored on the next rAF (preserves any existing transform; the 1px shift is invisible for one frame) тАФ re-rasterizing the layer regardless of content height. Belt-and-suspenders with the existing scroll nudge; both the focusout (force=false, keeps the mid-edit field-to-field skip) and visual-viewport-resize (force=true, the still-focused recurrence) paths get it. TDD: 3rd test in `viewport-scroll-guard.test.tsx` asserts the transform is set inside the repaint and cleared next frame. **Verified-by-checklist** (jsdom has no layout/WebKit; the unit test pins the DOM contract, the real proof is the operator's device test). If it recurs again: next levers are lowering the 80px keyboard-down threshold, nudging on `geometrychange`, or firing across more frames of the ~250ms keyboard slide.

**Recurrence #3 тАФ "hidden, not frozen" (2026-06-17):** operator reported the prior fix didn't take and reframed the symptom тАФ the screen feels **hidden** (pushed off), not blank/frozen. That's a DIFFERENT bug than the missing-repaint one. The layout comment (`app/layout.tsx`) says the guard's job is to "snap the locked document back to top тАж (otherwise the header is pushed off)", but `git log -S scrollTo` proves the guard **never** snapped to top тАФ only the net-zero nudge ever existed, so the header-pushed-off case was unhandled the whole time. Mechanism: body is locked (spec 64 overflow:hidden) so `window`/document scroll should always be 0, but iOS pans the document up to reveal a focused field near the bottom and doesn't always undo that pan on keyboard-close тЖТ the sticky header sits ABOVE the visible viewport ("hidden"). The net-zero nudge can't fix a real offset. **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now also `window.scrollTo(0, 0)` on keyboard-close (always safe тАФ a locked body cannot legitimately scroll; does NOT touch `<main>`'s own scroll = the reading position); gated by the same force/mid-edit logic so it never yanks the viewport while the keyboard is still up. TDD: +2 tests (snaps to top on close; does NOT snap while another field is being edited). **1001 unit** ┬╖ lint ┬╖ typecheck green. **Still NOT device-verified** тАФ operator must confirm on a real iOS PWA. If still seen: the pan may be a visual-viewport offset rather than a layout scroll тЖТ next lever is also zeroing `document.documentElement.scrollTop` / handling `visualViewport.offsetTop`.

**Recurrence #4 тАФ "BOTTOM BLANK GAP" тАФ actual root cause (2026-06-17):** prior two fixes (repaint, snap-to-top) didn't take. Diagnostic questions cracked it: operator says a **blank gap at the BOTTOM** (not top, not frozen), recovered by **rotate / navigate / refresh but NOT by scrolling**. That discriminator rules out both earlier hypotheses тАФ not a paint bug (scrolling would fix it), not a scroll offset (snap-to-top didn't fix it). It's a **stale viewport HEIGHT**: iOS standalone PWA leaves the locked body/`<main>` at the keyboard-REDUCED `100%`/`h-full` height after the keyboard closes, so the vacated bottom strip stays blank; rotate/navigate/refresh fix it because they all force a fresh relayout at the restored height. **Fix:** `repaintScroller` now also pins `document.body.style.height` to the live `window.innerHeight` for one frame, then releases it back to the CSS `h-full` lock on the next rAF тАФ replicating the rotate relayout so `100%` re-resolves against the correct viewport (own rAF, restores even with no `<main>`). TDD: +1 test (pins to innerHeight, clears next frame). **1002 unit** ┬╖ lint ┬╖ typecheck green. The guard now does FOUR things on keyboard-close: height relayout (bottom gap) ┬╖ window snap-to-top (hidden header) ┬╖ scroll nudge (blank scrollable) ┬╖ transform nudge (blank short form). **Still NOT device-verified** тАФ if the bottom gap persists, escalate from the one-frame toggle to PERMANENT height tracking (set body height = innerHeight on every resize/orientationchange, the `--app-height` pattern) rather than relying on iOS to recompute `100%`.

**Recurrence #5 тАФ STOP guessing, instrument (2026-06-17):** the height-relayout toggle (#4) also didn't take on device тАФ operator: "problem still there". Three blind fixes missed because the surface is auth-gated + iOS-PWA-only (no desktop repro), so the actual stale dimension is still unknown. Switched from guessing to MEASURING: new **`ViewportDebug`** flag-gated diagnostic overlay (`src/components/features/chrome/viewport-debug.tsx`, mounted in `app/layout.tsx`) тАФ renders null unless `?vpdebug=1` (or `localStorage.vpdebug="1"`), otherwise a fixed top-left overlay printing live `innerHeight / visualViewport.height/offsetTop/pageTop / documentElement.clientHeight / body.rectH / main.rectH+clientH+scrollH+scrollTop / window.scrollY / doc.scrollTop / activeElement`, updated on resize/scroll/orientationchange/focusin/focusout (+vv resize/scroll). Operator opens the broken screen with `?vpdebug=1`, closes the keyboard, screenshots the numbers in the blank-gap state тЖТ tells us which dimension is wrong тЖТ one definitive fix instead of a 4th guess. TDD: `viewport-debug.test.tsx` (renders null without flag; overlay with flag). **App-only, flag-gated, zero impact on normal use.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ test green. **TEMPORARY тАФ remove once the real fix lands.** The viewport-scroll-guard fixes (#2тАУ#4) stay in (each is harmless and may help one of the sub-cases); the next fix targets whatever the device numbers reveal.

**REGRESSION + REVERT (2026-06-17):** fixes #2тАУ#4 were NOT harmless тАФ operator: the orange р╕Цр╣Ир╕▓р╕вр╕гр╕╣р╕Ы hero bar (phase-uploader, `position: fixed inset-x-0 bottom-0`, a descendant of PageShell `<main>`) was "hovering all over the screen". Cause: the #4 `transform: translateY(1px)` nudge on `<main>` тАФ a CSS transform makes the element the **containing block for its `position:fixed` descendants**, so the fixed capture bar (and any fixed chrome: tab bar, modals, toasts) detaches from the viewport and tracks `<main>`. Worse, the guard's resize path fires `repaintScroller(true)` whenever `innerHeight - vv.height < 80`, which is TRUE even with no keyboard (vv.height тЙИ innerHeight), so incidental iOS visualViewport resize/scroll events re-applied the transform constantly тЖТ the bar wandered. **Action:** reverted `viewport-scroll-guard.tsx` + its test to the last-good pre-regression commit `4b90a3a` (436ee6f baseline = net-zero `scrollBy` nudge only); dropped the transform nudge, the `window.scrollTo(0,0)`, and the body-height pin (all three unverified, all three fired on the always-true gate). **The keyboard bug itself is still UNFIXED** тАФ but the guard is back to a known-good state and the regression is gone. The `ViewportDebug` overlay (af25bc5/6253d14) stays тАФ it's a fixed top-left `pointer-events:none` box that transforms no ancestor, so it's safe тАФ and it's now the path to a real diagnosis. **Lesson:** never put a `transform` on the scroll container тАФ it reparents all fixed chrome; and the `< 80` resize gate is too loose (fires without a keyboard), so anything behind it must be a true no-op when no keyboard is present.

**Recurrence #7 тАФ THE FIX (browser-verified) (2026-06-17):** operator sent the actual screenshots. They settled it: post-revert the orange bar is correctly bottom-fixed (regression gone), and the `/requests` create form is **physically clipped at the focused field (р╕Ир╕│р╕Щр╕зр╕Щ/р╕лр╕Щр╣Ир╕зр╕в) with a giant blank gap below** тАФ the scroller is collapsed to the keyboard-reduced height, not merely unpainted. Confirms the stale-height mechanism: the locked `html тЖТ body тЖТ main` chain is all `h-full` (=100%); iOS standalone shrinks the web view for the keyboard and never recomputes `100%` on close, so the root stays short тЖТ `<main>` clipped тЖТ blank gap. The #4 one-frame toggle failed because it released back to the broken `100%`. **Fix = new `AppHeightTracker`** (`src/components/features/chrome/app-height-tracker.tsx`, mounted in `app/layout.tsx`): pins `documentElement` + `body` height to the live `window.innerHeight` and re-applies on `resize` / `visualViewport resize` / `orientationchange` / `pageshow` / `focusout`(+100ms settle), so the keyboard-close resize restores the full height for real instead of relying on iOS to recompute `100%`. `<main>`'s `h-full` follows the corrected root. **No transform** (the regression lesson) тАФ only an explicit root height. **Browser-VERIFIED in the preview** (the first fix in this arc confirmed before shipping): at 812 the tracker pins html/body/main = 812px; shrink the viewport to 500 + fire resize тЖТ all track to 500; restore to 812 + fire resize тЖТ all restore to 812 (the keyboard openтЖТclose cycle); zero console errors. TDD: `app-height-tracker.test.tsx` (pins on mount; re-applies the restored height on resize). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1002 unit** (147 files) green. **Still owed: operator device confirmation** on the real iOS PWA (the in-app keyboard fires the resize/visualViewport events the preview can't), but the mechanism is now proven, not guessed. `ViewportDebug` overlay stays until confirmed, then both it and any vestigial guard nudge can be removed.

**Recurrence #8 тАФ wrong API found by research; corrected to visualViewport (2026-06-17):** #7 didn't work either, and web research (martijnhols.nl OSK article, htmhell viewport guide, quirksmode) explained why: **on iOS the soft keyboard is a SEPARATE LAYER тАФ the layout viewport never changes, so `window.innerHeight` stays constant and the window `resize` event does NOT fire when the keyboard opens/closes.** #7's `AppHeightTracker` was built on innerHeight + window resize, so it literally could not respond to the keyboard. Also confirmed: `interactive-widget=resizes-content` is NOT implemented in iOS Safari, so the declarative fix is out. **The only reliable signal is `window.visualViewport`** (`.height` shrinks; the `resize`/`scroll` events fire). **Rewrote `AppHeightTracker`** to publish `Math.round(visualViewport.height)` as the **`--app-vh`** CSS variable on `documentElement`, driven by `visualViewport.resize`/`scroll` (+ window resize/orientationchange/pageshow + focusout fallbacks + a 300ms delayed re-read for iOS's close-lag where visualViewport.height doesn't settle immediately). **PageShell's `<main>`** is now `style={{ height: "var(--app-vh, 100%)" }}` (h-full class stays as the pre-JS/non-iOS fallback) тАФ so the scroller tracks the actually-visible area: it shrinks above the keyboard and restores to full on close. CSS variable only тАФ **NO transform** (the reverted regression) and no scroll change, so fixed chrome is untouched. **Browser-VERIFIED in the preview:** at 812 тЖТ `--app-vh` 812px, main computed 812px; shrink the visual viewport to 500 тЖТ main 500 (the `visualViewport.resize` listener fires automatically, unlike window resize); restore to 812 тЖТ main 812; zero console errors. TDD: `app-height-tracker.test.tsx` rewritten with a mocked `window.visualViewport` (publishes --app-vh on mount; restores on visualViewport resize). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ test green. **Device pass still owed** тАФ the preview proves the wiring + the right API, but only the real iOS keyboard fires the genuine visualViewport events. If it STILL fails, enable `ViewportDebug` (corner├Ч5 / `?vpdebug=1`) and screenshot тАФ the live vv.height vs innerHeight numbers will confirm the model.

**Recurrence #9 тАФ THE ACTUAL CAUSE, from on-device numbers (2026-06-17).** Made `ViewportDebug` ON by default (#8.5) so the next screenshot carried the metrics. It did, and it ended six rounds of guessing. **Broken state, keyboard CLOSED (`active=body`): `window.scrollY = 389`, `document.documentElement.scrollTop = 389`** (also `vv.height=744`, `vv.offsetTop=49`, `doc.clientH=793`). The body is LOCKED (spec 64 overflow:hidden) so the document must always be at scroll 0 тАФ it was at **389**. iOS scrolls the documentElement to reveal the focused input when the keyboard opens and **does not reset it on close**, leaving the whole page shifted up ~389px = exactly the bottom blank gap. **Not paint, not main-height, not a stale 100% тАФ a stuck DOCUMENT scroll.** Every prior fix was the wrong axis; the `--app-vh`/visualViewport-height experiment (#8) even made `<main>` 49px short (744 vs the 793 layout) and is now REVERTED. **Fix:** `ViewportScrollGuard` rewritten to force `documentElement.scrollTop` / `body.scrollTop` / `window.scrollTo(0,0)` back to 0 when the keyboard is DOWN (`innerHeight - visualViewport.height < 100`, so it never fights iOS revealing the input mid-edit), fired on focusout + visualViewport resize/scroll + orientationchange/pageshow, with 300ms/600ms re-reads for iOS's late settle. Only the document scroll is touched тАФ `<main>`'s reading scroll is left alone; no transform, no height changes. Reverted: deleted `AppHeightTracker` + its test, removed it from layout, PageShell `<main>` back to plain `h-full`. TDD: guard test rewritten (resets doc scroll to 0 when keyboard down; does NOT reset when keyboard up). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ test green; preview confirms no regression (main full height, no console errors). `ViewportDebug` stays default-on for the confirmation screenshot тАФ expectation: in the broken-flow re-test, `window.scrollY`/`doc.scrollTop` now read 0. Remove the overlay once the operator confirms.

**Recurrence #10 тАФ THE REAL ROOT: iOS auto-zoom on input focus (2026-06-17).** After the doc-scroll-reset, operator: **"zooming out to 100% solves the blank portion."** That reframes it тАФ the page was ZOOMED IN, and the `scrollY=389` pan was the zoom-pan, not an independent document scroll. Cause confirmed in code: the form fields are NOT the `ui/input` primitive (which is `text-base`=16px on mobile) тАФ they're hand-rolled `<input>`/`<select>`/`type=date`/`<textarea>` at **`text-sm` = 14px** (`purchase-request-form.tsx` lines 201/222/241/297/308/358, and the same pattern across other forms). **iOS auto-zooms into any focused input whose font-size < 16px**, and a standalone PWA leaves the page zoomed + panned after blur тЖТ the blank portion; manual zoom-to-100% clears it. **Fix (design-preserving, global):** viewport meta now `width=device-width, initial-scale=1, maximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no` (Next `viewport` export: `maximumScale:1, userScalable:false`) тАФ a standalone PWA honors it and iOS no longer auto-zooms on focus, so the field-first `text-sm` sizes stay unchanged. **Trade-off:** disables pinch-zoom app-wide (acceptable for a native-feel ops PWA; operator framed zoom as the bug). Alternative if pinch-zoom must stay: bump every form input to 16px (enlarges field text, many files) тАФ offered to operator. Verified in preview: the rendered `<meta name=viewport>` content = `тАжmaximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no`; no console errors. Added `vv.scale` to `ViewportDebug` so the operator can confirm scale stays 1.00. The doc-scroll-reset (#9) stays as defense. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ test green.

## Spec 95 тАФ RESOLVED + cleanup (2026-06-18)

Operator confirmed on device: **no more zoom, no blank portion.** Root cause = iOS auto-zoom on `<16px` form inputs; fix = `maximum-scale=1` in the root `viewport` export (global тАФ the only `viewport` export in the app, so it covers every form on every screen). **Cleanup refactor:** removed the temporary `ViewportDebug` overlay (component + test + layout mount + corner-tap gesture); confirmed no dangling `--app-vh` / `AppHeightTracker` / `vpdebug` references remain. **Kept** `ViewportScrollGuard`, reframed as defense-in-depth for the spec-64 body-lock invariant (document scroll stays 0 if iOS scrolls the documentElement to reveal a focused input) тАФ a cheap no-op in the normal case; its header comment now points at `maximum-scale=1` as the primary fix. Browser-verified: overlay gone, viewport meta still `тАжmaximum-scale=1, user-scalable=no`, no `--app-vh` leftover, no console errors. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ test green. The spec-95 saga (#1тАУ#10) is closed; memory `ios-keyboard-repaint-freeze` rewritten to lead with the auto-zoom root cause + the process lesson (for device-only bugs: ship a metrics overlay first, get one screenshot, then fix the named dimension). **Possible follow-up (not done, scope):** the 14px hand-rolled inputs duplicate styling across forms тАФ a shared form-field input component would centralize the size decision and document the <16px-zoom trap.

## Spec 138 U1 тАФ р╕Хр╣Йр╕нр╕Зр╕Хр╕┤р╕Фр╕Хр╕▓р╕бр╕Фр╣Ир╕зр╕Щ urgent-follow-up panel (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1003 unit** (+4 new TDD) ┬╖ green. **Source:** a Claude Design (claude.ai/design) handoff bundle ("Mobile-first redesign for tablets", primary file `PRC Ops Worklist Mobile.dc.html`) тАФ the operator mocked the desktopтЖТmobile worklist UX and asked to implement the relevant aspects. Audit found most of the mock already shipped on the procurement `/requests` view (navy `AppHeader`, the 2├Ч2 `BuyerStat` KPIs, the spec-104 bands, `PoGroupCard`/`PhonePoBasket`, `BottomSheet`, `BottomTabBar`); the net-new affordances become spec-138 units. **U1 = the mock's headline addition:** a "р╕Хр╣Йр╕нр╕Зр╕Хр╕┤р╕Фр╕Хр╕▓р╕бр╕Фр╣Ир╕зр╕Щ" panel that lists the ACTUAL overdue deliveries (the items behind the р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф count, which until now was only a number/toggle), each row tapping into the request, with a footer link to the full overdue chase filter. **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/overdue-attention.ts` `selectOverdueFollowUp(rows, todayIso, limit=4)` тЖТ in-transit rows with `eta < today`, most-overdue first (earliest ETA, ties by PR number), capped, mapped to `{id, prNumber, itemDescription, supplier, eta, amount, overdueDays}`; "overdue" reuses `procurementBand(status)==='in_transit'` + `eta<today` so the set is exactly the spec-105 `procurementSummary.overdue` KPI's (4 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `OverdueFollowUpPanel` (server-safe, presentational, no `'use client'`): `bg-card`/`border-edge`/`rounded-card` panel, danger-toned header + count, each item a `<Link>` to `/requests/{id}` (danger dot ┬╖ PR-#### + item name [truncate, spec-47 card parity] ┬╖ supplier ┬╖ `р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щ N р╕зр╕▒р╕Щ` ┬╖ amount font-mono), footer `<Link>` "р╕Фр╕╣р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Фр╕Чр╕╡р╣Ир╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф" тЖТ the overdue filter. Field-First tokens only (danger trio + ink/edge/card) тАФ passes design-doctrine (no green-\*, no min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): computes `attentionItems` for procurement after the existing `amountById` admin read (the panel shows amount тАФ already back-office for procurement; no new query, no new money exposure), renders the panel in the procurement column right after the KPI grid, before the filters, only when non-empty; the footer href reuses `buildWorklistQuery({ ...filter, overdue: true })` (same target as the р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф tile). Named distinct from the existing `AttentionCard` callout primitive (different thing). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page тЖТ no preview; the pure helper carries correctness). Spec doc `138-mobile-worklist-redesign.md` (index updated; also backfilled the missing 136/137 index rows). **Next units (not started, one-per-session):** U2 KPI hero restyle (amber-gradient р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н tile + icons + tap-to-filter); U3 scrollable status-chip filter with live counts (replace the status `<select>`). **Refinement (2026-06-18, operator: "Remove р╕Хр╣Йр╕нр╕Зр╕Хр╕┤р╕Фр╕Хр╕▓р╕бр╕Фр╣Ир╕зр╕Щ section from phone view"):** wrapped the render in `hidden lg:block` so the panel now shows only on tablet/desktop (lg+) тАФ its origin in the design (the desktop `.dc.html` added it first, mobile mirrored). On the phone the р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф KPI tile (its filter toggle) stays the chase entry point; the panel read as redundant there. className-only, no logic/helper/test change; lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ test green.

## Spec 138 U2 тАФ desktop KPI hero row (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1007 unit** (+4 new TDD) ┬╖ green. **Source:** the SECOND file of the same Claude Design handoff тАФ the DESKTOP `PRC Ops Worklist.dc.html` (operator sent it after the mobile one). Click behaviour preserved тАФ only р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф is a filter toggle (the mock's clickable р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н/р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З tiles need the bandтЖТstatus filter mapping = U3, deferred). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page тЖТ no preview; the pure helper carries correctness; the side-by-side layout + tile styling are the operator's device/desktop look-feedback acceptance). Spec doc updated (U2 Change section). **Next (not started):** U3 status-chip filter with live counts (introduces the bandтЖТstatus mapping, then the р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н/р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З tiles can become filters too); sortable grid columns are a possible later unit (care: it overrides the priority/PO-contiguity order).

Removed the `BootTiming` boot-profiling overlay (commit 9d1008c) тАФ it was a TEMPORARY, default-on, top-left Navigation-Timing readout added to measure the operator-reported ~2s cold launch ("remove after measuring", same measure-first pattern as the spec-95 `ViewportDebug`). Re-adding to profile boot again is one `git revert 9d1008c`. The boot perf itself was NOT changed here (measurement tool only); if the ~2s persists, the fix targets whatever the captured Navigation-Timing split named (server auth round-trips vs redirect chain vs hydrate).

## Spec 138 U3 тАФ scrollable status-chip filter with live counts (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1015 unit** (+8 new TDD: 5 chip helper + 3 band axis) ┬╖ green. **U3 = the desktop/mobile mock's status chips.** Replaces the procurement worklist status `<select>` with a horizontally-scrollable row of BAND pills (р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Ф / р╕нр╕Щр╕╕р╕бр╕▒р╕Хр╕┤р╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з=`to_order` / р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З=`in_transit` / р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф=overdue), each with a LIVE count. **Introduces the band filter axis** the U2 seam flagged тАФ a single raw `status` enum can't express the in_transit band's purchased-OR-on_route set, so a later unit can now make the р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н/р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З KPI tiles tap-to-filter (reuse this axis). **Filter axis** (`src/lib/purchasing/worklist-filter.ts`): `ProcurementFilter` += `band: ProcurementBand | null`; `matchesProcurementFilter` AND-composes it (`procurementBand(row.status) === filter.band`); `buildWorklistQuery` serializes `?band=to_order|in_transit` (between project + status). The raw `status` axis STAYS (no UI now, but `?status=rejected` still surfaces the banded-out rejected/cancelled history тАФ the only escape, kept to avoid a regression). **Pure helper** `src/lib/purchasing/worklist-status-chips.ts` `buildWorklistStatusChips({rows, filter, todayIso})` тЖТ 4 chip descriptors `{key,label,count,href,active}` in order (all┬╖to_order┬╖in_transit┬╖overdue); counts reuse the spec-105 `procurementSummary` (so they agree with the KPI hero) + `all` = rows with a non-null band; `rows` is the supplier/project-narrowed set тЖТ counts are LIVE to those axes; each `href` reuses `buildWorklistQuery` preserving supplier/project/status while setting band+overdue per chip; `active` = overdue wins, else band, else all (5 unit tests, TDD). **Component** `WorklistStatusChips` (server-safe, presentational, no `'use client'`): `overflow-x-auto` `<Link>` pill row, each label + count badge, active pill `bg-fill/text-on-fill` тАФ mirrors the spec-137 `worklistChipClass`, min-h-11 tap floor, Field-First tokens only (passes design-doctrine: no green-\*/no-min-h-9). **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): parses `?band` (validated against `PROCUREMENT_BANDS`), builds the chips over supplier/project-narrowed rows, renders `<WorklistStatusChips>` between the KPI grid and the supplier/project pickers; `filterActive` += `filter.band`. Band filtering flows through `matchesProcurementFilter` + the existing `groupByProcurementBand` (an active band yields that one group); the `status` flat-group branch is untouched. **`ProcurementFilters`**: removed the status `<select>` (+ its now-dead `STATUS_OPTIONS` / label import / `PurchaseRequestStatus` type); supplier/project pickers stay; the р╕ер╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Хр╕▒р╕зр╕Бр╕гр╕нр╕З clear-all now also resets `band`. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page тЖТ no preview; pure helpers carry correctness; operator device/desktop look-feedback is acceptance). Spec doc updated (U3 Change section). **Open question (decided):** removing the `<select>` drops the only VISIBLE path to filter rejected/cancelled тАФ kept reachable via the `?status=` URL but no longer a control (spec-sanctioned; bands already drop those from the pipeline). **Spec 138 = U1┬╖U2┬╖U3 done.** Next possible unit: make the р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н/р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З KPI tiles tap-to-filter (the band axis is now in place); sortable grid columns (care: overrides priority/PO-contiguity order).

## Spec 138 U4 тАФ KPI hero tiles tap-to-filter (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1019 unit** (+4 net: the `worklist-kpis` test rewrote from 4 тЖТ 8 cases) ┬╖ green. U4 = the final mock affordance: the р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н (`to_order`) and р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З (`in_transit`) KPI hero tiles are now clickable band toggles (deferred from U2 "needs the bandтЖТstatus mapping", which U3 then built), reusing the U3 band axis. **Pure helper** (`src/lib/purchasing/worklist-kpis.ts`): `buildWorklistKpis` now takes `{ summary, outstanding, filter }` (dropped the pre-built `overdueHref`/`overdueActive` pair) and derives every tile's `href`/`active` internally via `buildWorklistQuery` тАФ mirroring the U3 `buildWorklistStatusChips` so the toggle logic lives in one place. A `bandTile(band)` helper builds the р╕гр╕нр╕кр╕▒р╣Ир╕Зр╕Лр╕╖р╣Йр╕н/р╕Бр╕│р╕ер╕▒р╕Зр╕Ир╕▒р╕Фр╕кр╣Ир╕З tiles as band toggles: `href` sets that band while clearing `overdue` (`band === key ? null : key` тАФ re-tapping the active tile clears it back to р╕Чр╕▒р╣Йр╕Зр╕лр╕бр╕Ф); `active = !filter.overdue && filter.band === key` (so neither band tile reads active while the overdue chase is on). The р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф tile is UNCHANGED тАФ exactly the existing chase toggle `buildWorklistQuery({ ...filter, overdue: !filter.overdue })` / `active = filter.overdue` / danger-when-`overdue > 0 || filter.overdue`; р╕Др╣Йр╕▓р╕Зр╕Ир╣Ир╕▓р╕в stays static (`href: null`). **`WorklistKpiTile`: no change** тАФ its `href`тЖТ`<Link>`+pressed-ring branch (U2 built it for the р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф tile) already covers the two new band tiles; the hot/shipping tones flow through it unchanged. **Page** (`src/app/requests/page.tsx`): passes `filter` to `buildWorklistKpis` (dropped the inline `overdueHref`/`overdueActive` args; `buildWorklistQuery` import stays тАФ the U1 panel footer still uses it); updated the stale "only the р╣Ар╕Бр╕┤р╕Щр╕Бр╕│р╕лр╕Щр╕Ф tile is a filter toggle" comment to note all three pipeline tiles toggle. 8 unit tests (TDD): order/captions, static tones, band-tile href+idle, active band toggles off, band hrefs preserve supplier/project + clear overdue, overdue chase toggle preserved, outstanding static, overdue tone. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated procurement page тЖТ no preview; the pure helper carries correctness; operator device/desktop look-feedback is acceptance). ## Spec 139 U1 тАФ App-feel slice 3: optimistic worker active-toggle (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1021 unit** (+2 new TDD) ┬╖ green. App-feel slice 3 (optimistic UI) was the last deferred slice тАФ done CAREFULLY per-surface, payroll-safe only. U1 = the worker active-toggle on `/workers` (`WorkerRow` in `src/components/features/labor/worker-roster-manager.tsx`), the clean safe pick: already a client component, the toggle reads `worker.active` only for presentation (name colour, `(р╕Ыр╕┤р╕Фр╣Гр╕Кр╣Йр╕Зр╕▓р╕Щ)` suffix, button label), nothing server-computed beside it. **Introduces React 19 `useOptimistic`** (was zero usage in the codebase). **Change:** replaced the toggle's `busy` + success-only `router.refresh()` with `committedActive = useState(worker.active)` (post-mount truth, advanced only by a successful flip) + `optimisticActive = useOptimistic(committedActive, (_, next) => next)` + `useTransition` (`isToggling`). `toggleActive` is now sync: inside `startToggle(async тАж)` it `setOptimisticActive(next)` (instant flip), awaits `updateWorker({ id, active: next })`, and on ok `setCommittedActive(next)` (commit тАФ the optimistic falls through to it when the transition ends, **no `router.refresh`** тЖТ the flicker is gone), on `!ok` `toast.error(result.error)` (the optimistic auto-reverts to `committedActive` = the rollback, now explained via the slice-1 toast channel тАФ the toggle previously failed **silently**). The row renders name colour / `(р╕Ыр╕┤р╕Фр╣Гр╕Кр╣Йр╕Зр╕▓р╕Щ)` suffix / button label from `optimisticActive`; button `disabled={isToggling}` (no double-fire race). **Untouched** (out of scope): the add-worker form + per-row edit-save stay pessimistic (they surface server validation + need the new/edited row to appear тЖТ keep `router.refresh`; `useRouter` import stays); the action + RPC + page unchanged; no styling/class change (design-doctrine green). **Tests** (`tests/unit/worker-roster-manager.test.tsx`, +2): optimistic flip-before-server + commit (deferred-promise: label flips to р╣Ар╕Ыр╕┤р╕Фр╣Гр╕Кр╣Йр╕Зр╕▓р╕Щ + `(р╕Ыр╕┤р╕Фр╣Гр╕Кр╣Йр╕Зр╕▓р╕Щ)` suffix appears while pending, `updateWorker({id:"w1",active:false})`, **no `router.refresh`**; resolve ok тЖТ stays flipped); rollback + toast (`{ok:false}` тЖТ reverts to р╕Ыр╕┤р╕Фр╣Гр╕Кр╣Йр╕Зр╕▓р╕Щ, suffix gone, `toast.error` fired, no refresh). The 4 existing notes/picker tests unchanged. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated pm/super `/workers` тЖТ no preview; the component tests carry correctness; operator on-device eyeball = acceptance, the slice-76/77/78 convention). Spec doc `139-app-feel-optimistic-ui.md` written (index row added; spec-138 index row relabelled to the full mock). **Next:** more payroll-safe optimistic surfaces (later units, per-surface); slice 5 motion (CSS list-enter via `@starting-style`; route View Transitions = guarded spike only). **Care recorded:** money/payment + labor-day logging surfaces are explicitly NOT optimistic candidates (server-computed / auditable).

## Spec 140 U1 тАФ App-feel slice 5: staggered list-enter motion (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration**; no db:push. lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1025 unit** (+4 new TDD) ┬╖ **build green** (CSS compiles тАФ ran `pnpm build` since CI/eslint don't cover globals.css). Pivoted to slice 5 (motion) after an investigation showed slice-3's remaining optimistic surfaces all carry desync (WP hold-status is a central field read across the page) or sensitivity (bank-change approval, consent) risk тАФ the worker toggle was the one uniquely-clean surface (operator chose the pivot). Slice 5 U1 = the **safe half** only: a CSS-driven, reduced-motion-gated staggered list-enter on the WP worklist (the most-opened screen). Route View Transitions stay a guarded spike (out of scope). **Pure helper** `src/lib/ui/list-enter.ts` `listEnterProps(index)` тЖТ `{ className:"list-enter", style:{"--enter-index": step} }` where `step = clamp(trunc(index), 0, LIST_ENTER_STAGGER_CAP=8)` тАФ the cap is load-bearing (a long list's tail must not wait seconds; rows past the cap share the last step), the testable seam (4 tests: index, clamp-0/negative, cap-tail, trunc-fraction, TDD). **`globals.css`** `@keyframes list-enter` (opacity 0тЖТ1, translateY 6pxтЖТ0) + `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference){ .list-enter{ animation: list-enter 240ms ease-out both; animation-delay: calc(var(--enter-index,0) * 35ms) } }` тАФ matches the spec-76 `toast-in` / spec-78 `sheet-up` pattern; `both` holds the row invisible through its stagger then settles visible; reduced-motion users get static fully-visible rows (the `from{opacity:0}` only applies while the animation is attached, which only happens inside the no-preference query тЖТ no FOUC). **`WorklistRow`** gained optional `enterIndex?: number` (merged into the root `<Link>` тАФ appends the class + sets the style var; omitted тЖТ every existing caller untouched). **`work-package-list.tsx`** passes `enterIndex={i}` from the map index at the 3 WorklistRow render sites (grouped-by-band / flat action-state / deliverable-group `<li>`) so the WP list glides in uniformly in every group mode. Server-safe (no `'use client'`; class+style are plain markup), zero data risk. **Verified-by-checklist + build** (auth-gated project page тЖТ no preview; the helper test carries the cap logic; CSS animation isn't unit-testable тЖТ operator on-device eyeball = acceptance, the slice-77 visual-slice precedent; design-doctrine green тАФ "list-enter" trips no green-\*/min-h-9 rule). Spec doc `140-app-feel-motion.md` written (index row added). **Next:** more lists (procurement worklist cards, contacts, suppliers, requests тАФ fast-follows reusing `listEnterProps`); route / View Transitions later (guarded spike, never the foundation); slice 3 optimistic continues per-surface (payroll-safe only).

## Spec 141 U1 тАФ equipment registry data layer (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1036 unit** (+11 new TDD validator) ┬╖ green; **pgTAP file 65 green post-push (22/22; full suite 65 files / 1363 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied migrations 20260723000000/000100/000200; `db:types` reconciled; `db push --dry-run` = remote up to date. First unit of the equipment tracking тЖТ intercompany rental system (ADR 0055; design + spec written and indexed earlier this session). **Owner-host decision (ADR 0055 open Q, operator-confirmed):** the equipment owner (sister company) lives in its OWN master `equipment_owners` тАФ not suppliers/service_providers тАФ so the future owner portal can bind to it the way the DC portal binds to contractors (ADR 0051); cost = a 3rd table this unit. **Migrations (3; ts 20260723000000/000100/000200; renumbered from a stale 20260713\* guess that collided with the already-applied PO-delivery migrations тАФ caught by db:push, nothing mis-applied):** `equipment_owners` (id/name/phone/created_by; masters posture тАФ staff SELECT, pm/procurement/super INSERT+UPDATE, no delete, created_by pin); `equipment_categories` (extensible lookup TABLE not enum per ADR 0055 ┬з2, optional parent_id self-FK, same posture); `equipment_items` тАФ the serialized-first registry: `equipment_tracking` enum (unit|bulk) + `equipment_status` enum (available/on_site/in_use/maintenance/returned/lost), owner_idтЖТequipment_owners + category_idтЖТequipment_categories (both NOT NULL), unique partial index on asset_tag (where not null), CHECKs enforcing unitтЗФno-qty / bulkтЗТqtyтЙе1 / bulkтЗТno-tag / name-nonblank / acq-costтЙе0. **MONEY POSTURE (ADR 0055 ┬з6, workers.day_rate pattern):** `acquisition_cost` + `acquired_at` get NO authenticated grant тАФ the column-scoped SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE grants OMIT them (admin-client-only read behind requireRole pm/super/procurement); both documented via `comment on column` (money-doc doctrine, file 38). Non-money columns field-visible (site_admin SELECT). **Policies eval-once-WRAPPED** `(select public.current_user_role())`/`(select auth.uid())` from day one (file-40 scanner). **No write RPC this unit** тАФ masters-pattern column-scoped grants + back-office policies + DB CHECKs carry the writes; money columns are simply unwritable by authenticated (capture lands later/P2). **Pure helper** `src/lib/equipment/validate-equipment-item.ts` `validateEquipmentItem` (decide-pattern union, Thai errors; 11 tests, TDD тАФ written FIRST, REDтЖТGREEN). **pgTAP file 65** (plan 22): catalog (3 tables + 2 enums + enum labels), id PK, RLS on all three, the 4 CHECK invariants (23514), money anti-grant (authenticated can't read acquisition_cost/acquired_at тЖТ 42501), staff read of non-money cols, site_admin INSERT denied (42501), pm happy INSERT, created_by-pin enforced (42501), no-delete (42501), anon denied (42501). **Types:** NOT hand-extended тАФ no U1 consumer; `db:types` regenerates authoritatively at the gate (avoids drift on a generated file). **Verified-by-checklist** (no UI this unit; the validator carries TS correctness, pgTAP carries DB correctness post-push). Docs: ADR 0055 + spec 141 + both indexes written earlier; memory `spec141-equipment-rental` saved. **Operator gate (DONE 2026-06-18):** branch тЖТ ff-merge тЖТ push (commits 303eebc feat + 2ec5cdc renumber + 6cac689 types); `db:push` applied; `db:test` green; `db:types` reconciled (app + worker copy); drift check clean. **Lesson:** check the true max migration timestamp before authoring тАФ the first `db:push` failed because 20260713\* (a stale-glob guess) collided with the already-applied PO-delivery run (20260713тАУ20260722); db:push caught it, nothing mis-applied, renumbered to 20260723\*. **Next units (P1 tracking, one-per-session):** U2 equipment list/detail UI; U3 `equipment_movements` append-only + current-location anti-join. Then P2 (money): U4 rental batches, U5 WP allocation + `freeze_wp_equipment_cost` (mirrors wp_labor_costs), U6 owner portal + WP-owner billing.

## Spec 141 U2 тАФ equipment management UI (2026-06-18)

Status: COMPLETE тАФ 2026-06-18. **App-only, no migration** (U1's grants/policies carry the writes). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1043 unit** (+7 new TDD: equipment-manager) ┬╖ green; pushed to main, Vercel auto-deploys. Makes the shipped registry visible/usable on-device. **Route** `/equipment` (`requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)` = pm/super/procurement, the U1 INSERT/UPDATE audience), mirrors `/workers` scaffold (PageShell + BottomTabBar + DetailHeader backтЖТ/settings). Reads via the **RLS server client only тАФ NO admin client** (no money on this surface: `acquisition_cost`/`acquired_at` stay admin-only, untouched). **`EquipmentManager`** (`src/components/features/equipment/`, new domain folder тАФ registered in feature-components-structure test): quick-add category + owner cards (bootstrap the masters), an add-item form, and per-item inline edit; the unit|bulk RadioChip swaps the asset-tag/quantity field; the U1 `validateEquipmentItem` gives Thai client-side errors before the action re-checks. No optimistic toggle (status = explicit edit, not a flip). **Actions** (`src/app/equipment/actions.ts`): `createEquipment` ┬╖ `updateEquipment` ┬╖ `createEquipmentCategory` ┬╖ `createEquipmentOwner` тАФ each `requireRole(BACK_OFFICE_ROLES)` (defense-in-depth + gives the caller id for the `created_by` pin), validates shape (`validateEquipmentItem` + UUID/enum), writes via the RLS client, decide-pattern returns, `revalidatePath`. **NO RPC** (unlike workers тАФ equipment's non-money writes have direct column-scoped grants). **Nav:** `SettingsLink` (р╕нр╕╕р╕Ыр╕Бр╕гр╕Ур╣М, Wrench icon) in the settings master-data section (pm/super) + `/equipment` added to `SETTINGS_TAB.match`. **Tests:** `tests/unit/equipment-manager.test.tsx` (7, TDD тАФ written FIRST: row render, add unit w/ tag, bulk-switch hides tag + passes qty, client-side reject before action, inline edit, quick-add category, quick-add owner); registered `/equipment` in the nav-back-affordance (STATIC_DETAIL) + feature-components-structure (ALLOWED_DOMAINS) anti-drift guards. **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page тЖТ preview only renders /login; the component test + U1 validator/pgTAP carry correctness; operator on-device pass = acceptance). Spec 141 doc gained a U2 section. **Seams / OUT (deferred):** money (acquisition_cost entry/display тАФ admin-only); movements/deployment (U3); site_admin read-only view + its nav (until U3 gives the field a reason); procurement nav entry (page reachable by URL today; settings master-data section is pm/super-gated); category/owner edit/delete (no-delete by design); a standalone detail page. **Next:** U3 `equipment_movements` (append-only, deploy-to-project per the Case-A model) + current-location anti-join; then P2 money units.

## Spec 141 U3 тАФ equipment movements + current location (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18** (commit/push/apply same as U1; commits a446dfe feat + bdd4e66 types/plan-fix). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1049 unit** (+5 new TDD: current-location helper) ┬╖ green; **pgTAP file 66 green post-push (16/16; full suite 66 files / 1379 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260724000000`; `db:types` reconciled; `db push --dry-run` = remote up to date. Data layer only тАФ the move-equipment UI is U4. **Migration** `20260724000000_create_equipment_movements.sql`: `equipment_movement_kind` enum (received/deployed/returned/maintenance/lost); `equipment_movements` (item_idтЖТequipment_items, kind, project_idтЖТprojects [null], quantityтЙе1, occurred_at, note, created_by) тАФ **CHECK** `project_id is not null тЗФ kind='deployed'` (a set deploys to a PROJECT, every other kind has no project; the Case-A attach-to-project model), **append-only** (SELECT+INSERT grants only, NO update/delete тАФ corrections are compensating movements), staff SELECT+INSERT (site_admin/pm/super/procurement тАФ the field moves gear; tracking not money) with created_by pin, eval-once-WRAPPED policies. **SECURITY DEFINER AFTER INSERT trigger** `equipment_movement_derive_status` denormalizes `equipment_items.status` from the new movement (receivedтЖТavailable, deployedтЖТon_site, returnedтЖТreturned, maintenanceтЖТmaintenance, lostтЖТlost) тАФ definer because site_admin records movements but can't UPDATE equipment_items under RLS (purchasing-derive-trigger posture). **Pure helper** `src/lib/equipment/current-location.ts` `currentEquipmentLocation` тЖТ latest movement per item by occurred_at тЖТ {kind, projectId} (the display source of truth; local enum union so it compiles pre-db:types; 5 tests, TDD first). **pgTAP file 66** (plan 15): catalog+enum, kindтЖФproject CHECK, qtyтЙе1, append-only (no update/deleteтЖТ42501), staff INSERT + created_by pin, visitor denied, the status-derive trigger (deployedтЖТon_site, returnedтЖТreturned), anon denied, staff-sees-2-movements. **Docs:** ADR 0055 ┬з4 reworded (event-log latest-OCCURRED wins, not a supersede anti-join; status-derive trigger noted) + spec 141 U3 section. **Money posture:** unchanged (movements carry no money; fully field-visible). **Seams:** status-derive trigger is last-recorded-wins (backdated movement sets status to its own kind); the helper is latest-OCCURRED + is the display SoT тАФ they agree when movements are recorded in order. Bulk current-on-project quantity (signed-sum) = allocations/P2. No audit_log row (the append-only log is the trail; no new audit_action enum). **Next:** U4 move-equipment UI (deploy/return actions + where-is-it display reusing the helper); then P2 money (U5 daily usage logs + freeze, U6 owner portal).

Spec 141 doc gained a U4 section. **Seams:** latest-event only (a bulk item split across projects shows its most recent deploy тАФ per-project on-hand tally is the P2 allocation concern); badge reads latest-OCCURRED while the status chip reads the trigger-derived last-RECORDED status (agree for chronological entry тАФ the U3 seam). **OUT (deferred):** site_admin field move view + nav + widened gate (next unit); backdating UI; movement edit/void (append-only тАФ correction = compensating movement); `in_use`; money; audit_log rows. **Next:** site_admin field move view (widen the gate, give the field its screen), then P2 money (U5 daily usage logs + freeze, U6 owner portal).

Spec 141 doc gained a U5 section. **Seams:** one `/equipment` surface, role-conditional (no field-route duplication тАФ WP-centric single-surface); RLS is the real guard, `canManageRegistry` is presentation only (registry actions re-check BACK_OFFICE_ROLES). **OUT / still deferred:** procurement nav entry (URL-only, the standing U2 deferral тАФ unchanged); registry edit for site_admin; money; movement edit/void; P2 money units. **Next:** P2 money тАФ U6 daily usage logs + `freeze_wp_equipment_cost` (mirrors wp_labor_costs), U7 owner portal + WP-owner billing; these need the operator's rate/billing inputs (ADR 0055 P2 open Qs).

Spec 142 + index written this session. **Verified-by-checklist** (no app UI this unit тАФ types regenerate authoritatively; pgTAP carries DB correctness post-push). **Seams / OUT:** no RLS policy change (the RPC is the PM path, INSERT policy stays super-only); contract_reference not accepted at create (deferred); `onboarding_dismissed_at` column lands in U3 where the checklist uses it. **Next:** U2 тАФ "New project" stub modal on `/projects` calling create_project (+ suggest as editable default) тЖТ redirect to the new project's detail page.

## Spec 143 U1 тАФ membership-scoped project visibility (ADR 0056) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1086 unit** ┬╖ green; **pgTAP file 70 green post-push (30/30; full suite 70 files / 1455 asserts, 0 failures)**; `db:push` applied `20260728000000`; `db:types` reconciled. Operator's 2nd ask: **project_manager + site_admin now see only the projects they're involved with** (a `project_members` row OR `project_lead_id`); **project_coordinator + super_admin see all**; **procurement keeps** its spec-102 cross-project read on projects/work_packages/purchase_requests. **Full RLS isolation** (operator decision) тАФ enforced on every project-scoped table, not just the hub. **ADR 0056** (amends 0013) written + indexed; spec 143 written + indexed. **Migration** `20260728000000_project_visibility_scope.sql`: two SECURITY DEFINER STABLE helpers тАФ `can_see_project(uuid)` (super/coordinatorтЖТtrue; pm/site_adminтЖТmember-or-lead; else false, never NULL тЖТ closes the [[rls-self-check-coalesce]] trap) and `can_see_wp(uuid)` (resolves WPтЖТproject, coalesce false); definer so they read project_members/projects/work_packages without re-triggering the policies that call them (ADR 0011, no recursion). **LeadтЖТmember backfill** in the same migration (idempotent `on conflict do nothing`) keeps current leads visible. **Rewrote 7 SELECT policies** (writes untouched), each preserving its exceptions: projects/work_packages = `procurement OR can_see_project`; deliverables = `can_see_project`; photo_logs/approvals = `can_see_wp`; purchase_requests = `requested_by=auth.uid() OR procurement OR can_see_wp` (separate appsheet_writer policy untouched); reports = `can_see_project AND role<>'site_admin'` (spec 19 тАФ reports still never reach site_admin). Eval-once wrapped form throughout (file 40). **Authoritative current policies pulled from pg_policies first** (not stale migration files) тАФ caught that spec 102 had added procurement to projects+work_packages. **pgTAP file 70** (plan 30): helper behaviour across 8 role/involvement combos + can_see_wp; projects/work_packages end-to-end per-row visibility (member sees, non-member doesn't, procurement keeps all, visitor none); child-policy wiring (qual references the right helper; reports excludes site_admin; PR keeps requester self-read). **Blast radius (expected тАФ access-model change):** 15 existing pgTAP suites hard-coded the old "PM/site_admin see everything" and broke on the read; fixed by seeding each fixture's PM/site_admin test users as project_members of its fixture project (scoped by test code + `auth.users.email` subquery тАФ `public.users` has no email column, the first attempt's trap). One ADR-0026 qual pin (file 17: "PR SELECT admits site_admin site-wide") was obsoleted by 0056 and re-pinned to the new `can_see_wp` gate. **No app code changed** тАФ RLS filters every cross-project list automatically (hub, worklists, payroll, reports). **ROLLOUT HEADS-UP (operator):** turning this on hides existing projects from PMs/site_admins who aren't a member/lead. Prod data is tiny (2 projects, 2 PMs, 3 site_admins; backfill enrolled the 1 lead) + super_admin sees all, so add the right people to each project via project settings (spec 80 UI). **Verified-by-checklist** (RLS is the SUT; pgTAP 70 + the regression suite carry correctness; operator on-device pass = acceptance). **Next:** U2 тАФ enable `project_coordinator` as a real role (roleHome тЖТ `/projects` + add to PROJECT_VIEW_ROLES so the see-all coordinator can actually browse; currently lands on /coming-soon). Then onboarding U5тАУU7 (WP seeding) remain.

## Spec 143 U3 тАФ complete the membership-scoping U1 missed (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1096 unit** ┬╖ green; **full pgTAP suite 73 files / 1489 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260731000000` + `20260732000000` (fix-forward, below); `db:types` reconciled. A **full-project-lifecycle audit** (4 parallel explorers) found U1's "full isolation" was incomplete: three project-scoped child tables still had role-level SELECT (cross-project leak to PM/site_admin), and the SELECT-only U1 left the app write policies role-level (a non-member could write a row for a project they can't read). **Migration `20260731000000`:** scoped `labor_logs` (staff SELECT only тАФ the bound-contractor self-read untouched) + `work_package_dependencies` SELECT via `can_see_wp`; mirrored the membership gate onto the **8 app write policies** (photo_logs INSERT, approvals INSERT, purchase_requests app-INSERT, deliverables INSERT+UPDATE, reports INSERT, work_packages UPDATE тАФ each KEEPS its role list so `project_coordinator` stays read-only; `labor_logs`/`wp_dependencies` are RPC-write-only so no write policy to mirror). Authoritative current policies pulled from `pg_policies` first (not stale files). **Self-inflicted regression + fix-forward:** `20260731000000` also tried to scope `photo_markups` (SELECTтЖТ`can_see_photo_log`, + helper). `photo_markups`' INSERT policy self-references `photo_markups` (tombstone-target EXISTS) тАФ safe only under a trivial role SELECT policy; under a function SELECT policy Postgres raised **42P17 infinite recursion** on every markup INSERT (caught by pgTAP files 31 + the test, but the migration was already pushed тЖТ prod markup-insert briefly broken). **`20260732000000`** reverts `photo_markups` to its original working policies + drops the unused `can_see_photo_log` тАФ prod un-broken. **pgTAP file 73** (plan 12): labor_logs + wp_dependencies + 7 write-mirror wirings + wp_dependencies end-to-end (member sees dep / non-member doesn't). **Two existing suites repaired:** `29-labor-capture` got a membership seed (its `correct_labor_log` id-lookup reads labor_logs as the PM тАФ now scoped); `31-photo-markups` returns to green once `photo_markups` reverted. **Verified-by-checklist** (RLS is the SUT; pgTAP 73 + the suite carry it). **Lesson:** scoping a SELECT policy on a table whose own write policy self-joins that table triggers 42P17 (ADR 0011 trap) тАФ needs a SECURITY DEFINER tombstone-target helper (pr_attachment_tombstone_target_ok precedent). **Remaining leak (documented, deferred to U4):** `photo_markups` is still role-level (low sensitivity тАФ drawing annotations). `labor_logs` (the sensitive one тАФ worker/day data) IS closed. **SPEC 143 now: U1 (RLS) + U2 (coordinator) + U3 (labor_logs/wp_dependencies + write-mirror) on prod; U4 (photo_markups via definer helper) is the one open item.**

## Spec 143 U4 тАФ scope photo_markups (recursion-free) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18. SPEC 143 COMPLETE.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1096 unit** ┬╖ green; **full pgTAP suite 74 files / 1501 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260733000000`; `db:types` reconciled. Closes the last read-leak the lifecycle audit found тАФ `photo_markups` was still role-level (cross-project). **Migration `20260733000000`:** recreates `can_see_photo_log(uuid)` (dropped in U3's revert) + adds `photo_markup_tombstone_target_ok(uuid,uuid)` тАФ a SECURITY DEFINER helper that performs the tombstone-target self-read (bypassing RLS, `auth.uid()` still the caller so the creator-pin holds). `photo_markups` SELECT тЖТ `can_see_photo_log`; INSERT keeps the content/own-tombstone rule but the tombstone branch now calls the helper instead of an inline `EXISTS (select тАж from photo_markups тАж)`, so the policy no longer self-joins the table тЖТ **no 42P17** (the U3 failure mode). Role list kept (coordinator read-only). **pgTAP file 74** (plan 12): both helpers exist + secdef, SELECT/INSERT wiring, and behaviourally the **exact content+tombstone INSERT that recursed in U3 now `lives_ok`** for a member, non-member sees 0 + INSERT denied, visitor sees 0. **Existing `31-photo-markups` (the comprehensive markup insert/tombstone/current-view suite) passes under the scoped policies** (its users are members via the U1 seed) тАФ strong real-world coverage that the recursion is gone and the member path is intact. **Lesson applied:** the pr_attachment_tombstone_target_ok pattern (definer helper for a self-referential append-only policy) is the canonical fix whenever such a table gets a function-based SELECT policy. **ADR 0056 "full isolation" is now actually complete** across all project-scoped tables (projects, work_packages, deliverables, photo_logs, approvals, purchase_requests, reports, labor_logs, work_package_dependencies, photo_markups); the only remaining write-side note is the labor RPCs (log_labor_day/correct_labor_log) gate on role + WP-status but not membership тАФ residual defense-in-depth (UI is read-locked for non-members), recorded, not exploitable.

## Spec 144 U1 тАФ defect rework: 'rework' status + reopen RPC (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1097 unit (+1: rework transition assert)** ┬╖ green; **full pgTAP suite 75 files / 1511 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260734000000` (enum) + `20260734000100` (RPC); `db:types` reconciled. Closes the lifecycle-audit gap "complete is terminal" тАФ operator: "complete doesn't mean defect-free." **Model (operator-chosen):** reopen to a new `rework` WP status (not reuse in_progress, not a punch-list entity); `complete тЖТ rework тЖТ re-capture After тЖТ pending_approval тЖТ complete`. **Reportable by site_admin + PM/super** (field finds defects; PM re-approves). **Migrations:** `rework` added to `work_package_status` in its OWN migration (PG: can't use a new enum value in the tx that adds it; `add value if not exists`), then `reopen_work_package_for_defect(p_wp,p_reason)` тАФ SECURITY DEFINER, role-gated (sa/pm/super) + membership-gated (`can_see_wp`, ADR 0056), complete-only, flips to `rework` + writes an `audit_log` row (`action='other'`, payload `{event:'wp_reopened_for_defect',reason}`, actor captured); non-complete/empty-reasonтЖТ22023, wrong-role/non-memberтЖТ42501. **Transition wiring:** `TRANSITIONABLE_FROM_STATUSES` += `rework` (after-photo from rework тЖТ pending_approval); labor already allowed in rework (log_labor_day blocks only complete); list-filter "outstanding" shows rework automatically; cost re-freezes on re-approve (existing path). **Enum-add ripple тАФ all consumers handled, typecheck-surfaced (the safety net):** `WORK_PACKAGE_STATUS_LABEL` (р╕Зр╕▓р╕Щр╣Бр╕Бр╣Йр╣Др╕В), `workPackageStatusPillClasses` (amber), `deriveActionBand` (reworkтЖТtodo) + `nextAction` (capture), `schedule-gantt` `STATUS_STYLE` (amber, pct 90; this one was caught only by typecheck тАФ a Record the grep would've missed). **pgTAP file 75** (plan 10) + enum pin (file 08) тЖТ six values. **Verified-by-checklist** (pure transition predicate unit-tested; RPC pgTAP'd; UI is U2). **Next:** U2 тАФ the "report defect" control on the WP page (complete-only, SA/PM/super) тЖТ reopen + show the rework status + reason from the latest audit row.

## Spec 144 U2 тАФ report-defect UI (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18. SPEC 144 COMPLETE.** App-only, **no migration** (reuses U1's `reopen_work_package_for_defect` RPC). lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1100 unit (162 files; +3 TDD: report-defect-control)** ┬╖ green. Makes the reopen path operable on the WP detail page. **Shipped:** (1) `reportDefect({projectId, workPackageId, reason})` action appended to the WP actions file тАФ validates reason (тЙд1000), calls the RPC under the user session (the SECURITY DEFINER RPC carries role+membership+complete-only), maps 42501тЖТ"р╣Др╕бр╣Ир╕бр╕╡р╕кр╕┤р╕Чр╕Шр╕┤р╣М (р╕Хр╣Йр╕нр╕Зр╣Ар╕Ыр╣Зр╕Щр╕Чр╕╡р╕бр╕Зр╕▓р╕Щ)" / 22023тЖТ"р╣Ар╕Йр╕Юр╕▓р╕░р╕Зр╕▓р╕Щр╕Чр╕╡р╣Ир╣Ар╕кр╕гр╣Зр╕Ир╣Бр╕ер╣Йр╕з", revalidates the WP + project paths; (2) `ReportDefectControl` ('use client', reuses `BottomSheet`: reason textarea тЖТ action тЖТ router.refresh, inline error); (3) WP page wiring тАФ when `status='complete'` show the report-defect button (all WP-page viewers are SA/PM/super; the RPC re-gates membership), when `status='rework'` show an amber `AttentionCard` with the latest defect reason (fetched from the newest `wp_reopened_for_defect` audit_log row via `payload->>event` filter). From rework, the existing captureтЖТapprove cycle resumes (after-photoтЖТpending_approval wired in U1; cost re-freezes on re-approve). **Verified-by-checklist** (auth-gated page тЖТ /login in preview; the 3 component tests cover submit-gate/success-refresh/error; the RPC is pgTAP'd in file 75; operator on-device = acceptance). **SPEC 144 COMPLETE (U1 DB + U2 UI): a defect on a completed WP now reopens it to `rework`, captures the reason, and flows back through capture тЖТ re-approval тЖТ complete.** Closes the lifecycle-audit "complete is terminal" gap.

## Spec 145 тАФ lock new work on a completed project (warranty-aware) (2026-06-18)

Status: **SHIPPED to prod тАФ 2026-06-18.** lint ┬╖ typecheck ┬╖ **1100 unit** ┬╖ green; **full pgTAP suite 76 files / 1521 asserts, 0 failures** post-push; `db:push` applied `20260735000000`; `db:types` reconciled. Operator decision: a project locks once completed тАФ **caveat:** completion тЙа full closure (client retains a warranty %, Buildall 5% for a year; defects surface in that window), so the lock blocks NEW work but must NOT block warranty defect-rework. **Enforcement = a `BEFORE INSERT` trigger on `work_packages`** (`work_packages_block_insert_on_closed_project`, raises `P0002`) gated by `project_is_open(uuid)` (status in active/on_hold) тАФ **one chokepoint** over every WP-creation path (manual/template/copy/CSV) without re-typing the 3 creation RPCs, and **INSERT-only** so spec-144 reopen-to-rework (an UPDATE) + the rework WP's capture/labor/approval cycle keep working on a completed project (the warranty carve-out falls out for free; gates on WP status, not project status).
